title: The Essential Evangelion Chronicle Side A and Side B
display_title: The Essential Evangelion Chronicle Side A and Side B
wikitext:
[]: The Essential Evangelion Chronicle: Side A & Side B             Quote Post by COACH » Wed Sep 08, 2010 5:21 pm Did anybody ever actually buy these two books? The Evageeks Wiki has no information about them, other than the release dates. Any idea on how many pages of the original 960 paged Evangelion Chronicle were reprinted here? I saw both books at my local Kinokuniya, but the price was too high for a blind buy. Especially because I'm mainly interested in owning a physical copy of the 35 Evangelion Proposal pages. Does either book contain all of them? If not, which of the original 30 issues of Evangelion Chronicle contains the Proposal stuff?

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Wed Sep 15, 2010 10:25 am Reichu scanned them, so why not ask here where in the Evangelion Chronicle they were located?

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Wed Sep 15, 2010 7:28 pm Well, I'd rather find out if the pages I'm looking for are featured in Essential or not. The individual Evangelion Chronicle issues are probably harder to track down... Nobody ever bought these things, though?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Sun Sep 19, 2010 9:32 am You'll need to find issues 1-24 of Eva Chronicle if you want every page of the proposal. And the place I bought my copies from is long sold out. In other words, you're stuffed.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Sun Sep 19, 2010 5:33 pm Those sneaky bastards... I figured they'd split them up like that. Have you confirmed that Essential A and B don't have the Proposal pages, though?

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Mon Sep 20, 2010 4:22 am I have no idea what's in those books, but I always assumed that they included everything that's in chronicle. Here's the best description I could find: [url]http://www.hlj.com/product/SON77018[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Mon Sep 20, 2010 1:39 pm 128 pages x 2 books is 256 pages. Chronicle was 960 pages. I'd just look at the books at Kinokuniya, except all of their books are wrapped in plastic. Bastards...

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Tue Sep 21, 2010 9:06 am Normally you can ask to have a look at the books that are wrapped in plastic, unless they're wrapped by the publishers (well, at least you can at my Kino).

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Tue Sep 21, 2010 9:14 am Fazmotron wrote: Normally you can ask to have a look at the books that are wrapped in plastic, unless they're wrapped by the publishers (well, at least you can at my Kino).

[]:             Quote Post by Fazmotron » Tue Sep 21, 2010 9:26 am Well, the only time I asked I ended up buying the book, so I don't know what they do with it if you don't want to buy it.

[]:             Quote Post by Riotgear » Sat Sep 25, 2010 12:46 pm You're lucky COACH , because my anime-related books are some of the few of my possessions that I know the location of. When I was packing, preparing to move, I knew I'd want to have access to many of my books so I put them all together and marked those boxes clearly. So I looked through the boxes, found Side A & B, cut them out of the bubble wrap I'd put wrapped them in over two years ago and took a quick look through them. Unfortunately neither book contains the proposal pages (oh, uh sorry. I guess you're not that lucky). Both books are divided into the same three sections - "Character and Evangelion", "Episode Guide and Checkpoint" and "Goods Collection". Side A covers episodes 1-14 and Side B covers 15-26 and EoE. Did you want to know anything else about them before I seal them back up and lock them away again? :P I have the original 30 issues and the "Evangelion Chronicle Illustrations" book as well. I'd sorta prefer not to have to unwrap all the individual issues but if there was something you really desperately needed to know that you couldn't find out online I might be persuaded.

[]:             Quote Post by COACH » Mon Sep 27, 2010 1:21 am Nope, that will be all! Oh well... Perhaps I'll try to hunt down the entirety of Chronicle in the near future. (Don't know if it's possible anymore, though.) I kinda wanted to buy it for awhile anyway. Thank you!

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Sep 29, 2010 3:16 pm I got them while in London in 2008. Were quite expensive, considering how expensive quids were relative to other currencies those days (before the Lehman thingie). Never browsed them in detail though, but now during my efforts to move to a new home I took a more detailed look at them and they're cool. Don't think they have essentially anything that the individual "chronicles" did not, but it's a nice compilation to have.

[]:             Quote Post by Gib » Wed Jan 19, 2011 11:42 am Are these books mere episode guides? Are they useful? Don't scanlations exist? Where can I see any pages? I think that Glénat published these books in french last year.

[]:             Quote Post by AchtungAffen » Wed Jan 19, 2011 1:16 pm These books contain an abridged version of the complete Evangelion Chronicle. They aren't just episode guides, they contain various other info.

[]:             Quote Post by cyharding » Fri Jul 13, 2012 10:58 pm I bumped this thread because it looks like the books are going to be released in English this November. Wondering if these books are worth it?

[]:             Quote Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Fri Jul 13, 2012 11:42 pm I'll try to get ahold of them for art purposes. For my works on Eva Xover ga Fanfics and for my fan work on Rommels H fic he wrote.

[]:             Quote Post by gwern » Sat Jul 14, 2012 8:10 am cyharding wrote: I bumped this thread because it looks like the books are going to be released in English this November. Wondering if these books are worth it?

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Jul 14, 2012 11:50 am I'm Definitely picking this up when it comes out.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sat Jan 19, 2013 2:54 am Just to bump this thread: http://www.evageeks.org/2013/01/udon-entertainment-releasing-evangelion-chronicle-illustrations/ Christopher Butcher from Udon Entertainment contacted the site and said they're still planning on releasing Side A and Side B this year.
